# Conventions

## Event-only projects are invisible in the UI

[Trigger]: When a user attempts to view a project that only contains events. [Detail]: The ingestion pipeline explicitly excludes projects that have no associated static content (files, agents, skills) from the main projects list. Consequence: Users cannot accidentally interact with or delete ephemeral event streams, but they also cannot easily inspect the raw event data for such projects without querying the database directly.

_Source: `src/ingest/sync.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `database`_

## Event-only projects excluded from the projects list

[Trigger]: When querying the list of active projects for UI rendering or API endpoints. [Detail]: Projects that only emit events without maintaining a persistent project record in the `projects` table are filtered out of the main project list. They exist solely as transient data sources. [Consequence]: Users cannot browse or manage these ephemeral projects via the standard interface, and their knowledge entries are not attributed to a named project in the main dashboard, reducing traceability for temporary analysis sessions.

_Source: `src/knowledge/scan.js`_

_Tags: `api`, `backend`_

## Event-only projects are excluded from the projects list

[Trigger]: When querying `GET /api/projects`. [Detail]: The test suite explicitly asserts that the API excludes projects that have events but no associated git repository (`.git` directory missing). The logic filters to only list `cl_projects` which represent git repos. Consequence: If a user ingests logs from a non-git directory (e.g., a local temp folder), those events are processed but the directory will not show up in the project list, potentially confusing users expecting to see all data sources.

_Source: `test/routes/routes.test.js`_

_Tags: `testing`, `api`_
